title: LC-meta Minimal Administrative and Learner Metadata
description: Minimal proof-of-concept schema for integrating the administrative and learner metadata blocks of LC-meta into the metadata editor. This package is derived from the first and third LC-meta data sheets provided for the repository test.

sections.title: Corpus Metadata - Administrative Metadata
sections.description: Administrative metadata block from the LC-meta core metadata schema for learner corpora.

fields.title: Full name of the corpus
fields.description: Full name of the corpus. Example: International Corpus of Learner English, with multiple entries for different names in different languages.

fields.title: Corpus acronym
fields.description: Corpus acronym. Example: ICLE.

fields.title: Corpus version
fields.description: Corpus version. One possible approach is major.minor.patch, where major indicates new data or annotations and minor indicates corrections.

fields.title: Persistent identifier
fields.description: Persistent identifier for the corpus. Typically a DOI or Handle, for example http://hdl.handle.net/20.500.12124/25.

fields.title: Corpus citation
fields.description: Recommended citation for the corpus. Example: Author(s) or Responsible(s) (Year). Title of the data set (Version number): Distributor, Identifier or Location.

fields.title: Reference article
fields.description: Reference articles typically contain an extensive verbal description of the corpus and its design.

fields.title: Short description of the corpus
fields.description: Short description of the corpus.

fields.title: Link to more extensive corpus documentation
fields.description: Link to more extensive corpus documentation. Example: https://www.porta.eurac.edu/lci/leonide/.

fields.title: Corpus creator
fields.description: Corpus creator. Example: Max Musterman.

fields.title: Corpus contributor
fields.description: Corpus contributor. Example: Jane Doe (transcription of English texts).

fields.title: Contact e-mail address
fields.description: Contact e-mail address. Example: porta@eurac.edu.

fields.title: Name of related research project
fields.description: Name of related research project. Example: MERLIN.

fields.title: Link to related research project
fields.description: Link to related research project. Relevant when a related research project is specified. Example: https://merlin-platform.eu/.

fields.title: Person or entity responsible for the corpus
fields.description: Person or entity responsible for the corpus. Example: Institute for Applied Linguistics, Eurac Research.

fields.title: Licensing terms and conditions
fields.description: Access category for the corpus.

fields.title: Access requirements
fields.description: Access requirements when availability is restricted. Example: academic use.

fields.title: Licence name
fields.description: Licence name. Example: ACA-BY-NC-NORED.

fields.title: Link to licence text
fields.description: Link to the licence text. Example: http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/.

fields.title: Date of publication
fields.description: Date of publication. Accepts YYYY, YYYY-MM, or YYYY-MM-DD.

fields.title: Associated version
fields.description: Associated version. Example: 1.0, link to version.

fields.title: Character encoding
fields.description: Character encoding. Example: utf-8, latin, ascii.

fields.title: File format
fields.description: File format. Example: txt, csv, tiff, xml, JSON.

fields.title: File format specification
fields.description: File format specification. Example: TEI P5 or corpus-specific format manuals.

sections.title: Learner Metadata
sections.description: Learner metadata block from the LC-meta core metadata schema for learner corpora. In this proof of concept the learner sheet is represented as a flat corpus-level block; the optional 3.4 L2 profile is captured as parallel repeatable fields rather than nested learner-language objects.

pages.title: Learner record
pages.description: Identifiers for the learner metadata record used in this proof of concept.

fields.title: Unique identifier
fields.description: Unique identifier for the learner record represented in this metadata entry.

fields.title: Age at text production
fields.description: Age at text production.

fields.title: Gender
fields.description: Gender.

fields.title: Highest level of education
fields.description: Highest level of education.

fields.title: Socio-economic status
fields.description: Description of the learner's perceived socio-economic status. For example, the CHAT format uses working class, middle class, upper class, and limited income.

fields.title: Country
fields.description: Country of the learner. Use ISO 3166 country codes.

fields.title: Region
fields.description: Geographical details about the learner if relevant to their language use.

fields.title: Learner environment
fields.description: Learner environment, for example urban or rural.

fields.title: Does the learner have an impairment?
fields.description: Whether the learner has an impairment.

fields.title: Learner impairment
fields.description: Type of impairment when relevant. Examples include hearing impairment, speech impairment, vision impairment, or other impairments. Use tuples if you need to add conditions such as permanence.

fields.title: Is the learner characterized by atypical learning or atypical (cognitive) development?
fields.description: Whether the learner is characterized by atypical learning or atypical cognitive development.

fields.title: Type of atypical learning or atypical development
fields.description: Examples include speech sound disorder, language disorder, dyslexia, or attention deficit hyperactivity disorder.

pages.title: Target language: learning context, exposure and proficiency
pages.description: Target-language learning context, exposure, and proficiency. LC-meta conditions that depend on corpus-level design metadata are preserved in the field descriptions and kept optional until the related design sheet is modeled.

fields.title: Learner target language
fields.description: Target language of the learner. Use an ISO 639-3 language code.

fields.title: Was the learner in an instructed learning context at the time of data collection?
fields.description: Whether the learner was in an instructed learning context at the time of data collection.

fields.title: Was the learner in an immersive instructed learning context at the time of data collection?
fields.description: Whether the learner was in an immersive instructed learning context at the time of data collection. Language immersion means the target language is primarily used during teaching.

fields.title: Was the learner in a country or region where the target language is spoken at the time of data collection?
fields.description: Whether the learner was in a country or region where the target language is spoken at the time of data collection.

fields.title: Cumulated time spent in a target-language environment (months)
fields.description: Cumulated time spent in an environment where the target language is spoken, in months.

fields.title: Age of onset of acquisition
fields.description: Age of onset of acquisition of the target language.

fields.title: Learning context of target language from onset to current age
fields.description: Learning context of the target language from onset to current age. Use tuples and choose among instructed, immersive, and naturalistic, for example {12-20: instructed}.

fields.title: Context of use of the target language from onset to current age
fields.description: Context of target-language use from onset to current age. Typical contexts include family, friends, leisure, public, work, and education. Use tuples if you need to preserve time ranges.

fields.title: Listening experience
fields.description: Listening experience in the target language.

fields.title: Speaking experience
fields.description: Speaking experience in the target language.

fields.title: Reading experience
fields.description: Reading experience in the target language.

fields.title: Writing experience
fields.description: Writing experience in the target language.

fields.title: Social media experience
fields.description: Social media experience in the target language.

fields.title: Learner proficiency
fields.description: Learner proficiency result, for example a language-test score, in-house test result, or self-assessment. LC-meta marks this as obligatory when learner-centred proficiency assignment is available in the corpus design metadata.

fields.title: Learner proficiency (CEFR)
fields.description: Learner proficiency converted to CEFR where possible. LC-meta ties this to learner-centred proficiency assignment in the corpus design metadata.

fields.title: Official language test score
fields.description: Official language-test score when the corpus production setting is official language testing.

fields.title: Learner L1
fields.description: Learner L1. Use ISO 639-3 language codes. L1 often refers to the language or languages learned from birth or infancy; use corpus documentation to clarify the interpretation if needed.

fields.title: Parent L1
fields.description: Parent L1. Use tuples such as [mother, eng] or [father, spa] if you need to preserve parent-language pairs.

fields.title: Language spoken at home
fields.description: Language spoken at home. Use tuples such as [ita, 80%] if you need to preserve proportions.

fields.title: Language used with friends
fields.description: Language used with friends. Use tuples such as [eng, 30%] if you need to preserve proportions.

fields.title: Language of education (kindergarten)
fields.description: Language of education in kindergarten. Use tuples if you need to preserve proportions or multiple languages.

fields.title: Language of education (primary school)
fields.description: Language of education in primary school. Use tuples if you need to preserve proportions or multiple languages.

fields.title: Language of education (secondary school)
fields.description: Language of education in secondary school. Use tuples if you need to preserve proportions or multiple languages.

fields.title: Language of education (higher education)
fields.description: Language of education in higher education. Use tuples if you need to preserve proportions or multiple languages.

pages.title: Other languages: learning context, exposure and proficiency
pages.description: Optional and repeatable set of variables for languages known by the learner other than L1 and the target language. In this proof of concept, these values are stored as parallel repeatable fields rather than nested per-language objects.

fields.title: L2s that are not the target language
fields.description: Other L2s known by the learner. Use ISO 639-3 language codes.

fields.title: Age of onset of acquisition
fields.description: Age of onset of acquisition for each learner_L2 value.

fields.title: Learning context from onset to current age
fields.description: Learning context for each learner_L2 value. Use tuples and choose among instructed, immersive, and naturalistic.

fields.title: Context of use from onset to current age
fields.description: Use context for each learner_L2 value. Typical contexts include family, friends, leisure, public, work, and education. Use tuples if you need to preserve time ranges.

fields.title: Listening experience
fields.description: Listening experience for each learner_L2 value.

fields.title: Speaking experience
fields.description: Speaking experience for each learner_L2 value.

fields.title: Reading experience
fields.description: Reading experience for each learner_L2 value.

fields.title: Writing experience
fields.description: Writing experience for each learner_L2 value.

fields.title: Social media experience
fields.description: Social media experience for each learner_L2 value.

fields.title: Learner proficiency
fields.description: Learner proficiency result for each learner_L2 value, for example a language-test score, in-house test result, or self-assessment.

fields.title: Learner proficiency (CEFR)
fields.description: Learner proficiency converted to CEFR for each learner_L2 value.

fields.title: Cumulated time spent in an L2 environment (months)
fields.description: Cumulated time spent in an environment where each learner_L2 is spoken, measured in months.

pages.title: Other individual differences
pages.description: LC-meta links these variables to corpus-level test availability, so they remain optional until the related design metadata is modeled in the package.

fields.title: Motivation test score
fields.description: Motivation test score. Use tuples if necessary.

fields.title: Attitude test score
fields.description: Attitude test score. Use tuples if necessary.

fields.title: Aptitude test score
fields.description: Aptitude test score. Use tuples if necessary.

fields.title: Any comments regarding this learner
fields.description: Any comments regarding this learner, for example a learner biography or more detail about the multilingual profile.
